user.title: Samsung Air Conditioner (DPLUG / 2878)
user.description: How would you like to set up your air conditioner?

menu_options.onboard: Connect a new unit to Wi-Fi (instructions)
menu_options.manual: It is already on my Wi-Fi (enter IP)

onboard.title: Connect the unit to your Wi-Fi
onboard.description:
Old Samsung units are not joined to Wi-Fi over the network protocol — do it once, physically.

**Option A — WPS (easiest, recommended)**
1. Make sure the unit is **not** in AP mode.
2. On the remote, **hold `Settings` for 4 seconds** (hidden function: the display only blinks) to start WPS.
3. Within 2 minutes, **press the WPS button on your router**.
4. When the Wi-Fi LED on the module stays solid, it has joined.

**Option B — from a computer (if your router has no WPS button)**
1. Install **Python 3** if you don't have it (https://www.python.org/downloads/), then **download the helper script** (a single file, no extra installs needed): https://github.com/porech/samsung_ac_dplug/blob/main/scripts/provision.py
2. On the remote, **hold `Timer` for 4 seconds** to put the unit in AP mode. It creates a Wi-Fi network named **`SMARTAIRCON`** (password **`1111122222`**).
3. **Connect this computer to the `SMARTAIRCON` network.**
4. **Run the script** — `python3 provision.py` — and, when prompted, enter your home Wi-Fi name and password. (Advanced users: `python3 provision.py --help` shows options to run it without prompts.)
5. When it prints **Done**, the unit joins your Wi-Fi; reconnect this computer to your normal network.

When the unit is on your Wi-Fi, press **Submit**. If you enabled DHCP discovery it may already have been found automatically; otherwise enter its IP on the next screen.

manual.title: Connect to the air conditioner
manual.description: Enter the unit’s IP address. The token is handled on the next step.

token.title: Acquire the access token
token.description:
The unit at {host} only issues a token at power-on (a physical proof-of-access step).

1. **Turn the air conditioner OFF** with the remote, if it is on.
2. Press **Submit** below (leave the field empty).
3. Within ~30 seconds, **turn the air conditioner ON** with the remote.

The token will be captured automatically and stored.

**Alternatively**, if you already have this unit's token, enter it below and press Submit.

save_token.title: Token acquired
save_token.description:
Done — the token for the unit at {host} was acquired and saved in this integration.

💡 **Tip:** save this device's token so you don't have to acquire it again in the future (you can paste it directly when adding the unit):

```
{token}
```

Press **Submit** to finish.

reauth_confirm.title: Re-authenticate Samsung air conditioner
reauth_confirm.description: The token for the unit at {host} was rejected (it can change after a module reset). Acquire a new one: turn the unit OFF, submit (leave empty), then turn it ON within ~30s. Or enter a known token below.

discovery_confirm.title: Samsung air conditioner found
discovery_confirm.description: A Samsung air conditioner was discovered at {host}. Set it up now? In the next step you will acquire a token (power-on), unless you already know it.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect. Check the IP address and that the unit is on the network.
error.auth: The token was rejected. Leave it empty to acquire a new one.
error.no_token: No token received. Make sure the unit was OFF, then turned ON within the window. Try again.

abort.already_configured: This air conditioner is already configured.
abort.not_samsung_ac: This device does not speak the Samsung DPLUG protocol.

data_description.live_updates: Keep one persistent connection for instant push updates. If off, the integration polls and uses no persistent connection (more resilient).
data_description.scan_interval: Polling interval when live updates are off; fallback/keepalive poll when they are on.

get_schedules.name: Get schedules
get_schedules.description: List the schedules stored on the unit's built-in scheduler. This controls the air conditioner's built-in scheduler, which runs even when Home Assistant is offline and only switches the unit on or off at a set time. For more complex scenarios (temperature, mode, fan…) use Home Assistant automations.

set_schedule.name: Set schedule
set_schedule.description: Create or edit an on/off schedule. This controls the air conditioner's built-in scheduler, which runs even when Home Assistant is offline and only switches the unit on or off at a set time. For more complex scenarios (temperature, mode, fan…) use Home Assistant automations.

time.name: Time
time.description: Local time of day when the schedule fires.

power.name: Power
power.description: Whether the unit turns on or off.

repeat.name: Frequency
repeat.description: How often it runs: once, every day, or weekly on the selected days.

days.name: Days
days.description: Which days to run on — required for 'Once' and 'Weekly', ignored for 'Every day'.

schedule_id.name: Schedule ID
schedule_id.description: ID of an existing schedule to edit; leave empty to create a new one.

delete_schedule.name: Delete schedule
delete_schedule.description: Delete a schedule from the unit's built-in scheduler. This controls the air conditioner's built-in scheduler, which runs even when Home Assistant is offline and only switches the unit on or off at a set time. For more complex scenarios (temperature, mode, fan…) use Home Assistant automations.

schedule_id.name: Schedule ID
schedule_id.description: ID of the schedule to delete (see the Schedules sensor or get_schedules).
